data.title: ESAB announces offering of $1B senior notes
data.description:
• ESAB (ESAB) said on Thursday that it intends to offer $1B of senior notes due 2031.
  • The company said it intends to use net proceeds from the notes sale to fund part of the purchase price for the acquisition of Eddyfi Holding and related entities.
  • The notes will be guaranteed by certain of its current and future domestic restricted subsidiaries.

data.title: Canada posts C$3.65B trade deficit in January
Justera appoints Andrew Ryu as interim CEO
data.description:
• Justera Health (SCRSF) has appointed Andrew Ryu as its interim chief executive officer and chairman of the board while the company advances its operational and corporate initiatives.
  • Ryu is a graduate of McMaster University and the University of Toronto. He was the founder and CEO of Loyalist Group (LOY), which was recognized as a top TSXV Company in 2013 and ranked fourth and named pick of the street in 2014. He later served as CEO and chairman of Datametrex (DM) until May 2022.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: JFB pushes 2-for-1 stock split date before XTEND AI Robotics listing
data.description:
• JFB Construction (JFB) updated the timing of its planned 2-for-1 stock split of issued and outstanding common shares from March 20 to March 24 as part of its $1.5B all-stock merger with XTEND.
  • This split will be carried out for shareholders of record as of March 23, 2026, with investors receiving one additional share for every share held.
  • Trading of the common stock is expected to begin on a split-adjusted basis after market close on March 23 on Nasdaq under the ticker JFB.
  • After the stock split, the outstanding shares will increase to about 14.0M from roughly 7.0M, with no change to the stock’s par value.
  • Joseph F. Basile III, CEO of JFB, said the stock split aims to lower the per-share price and increase the outstanding shares to broaden investor access ahead of the planned XTEND AI Robotics (XTND) NASDAQ listing.
  • JFB Construction Holdings (JFB) shares traded lower to around $17.54 in premarket hours.

data.title: Oddity Tech approves $200M stock buyback program
data.description:
• Oddity Tech (ODD) announced on Thursday that it has approved a share buyback program authorizing the repurchase of up to $200M of its Class A ordinary shares.
  • Under the new plan, the company will replace its earlier $150M buyback program, with the authorization set to expire on March 31, 2029, or when funds are fully used.
  • The company had repurchased about $97M under its prior buyback authorization, including about $50M year-to-date.
  • Shares +3.63%.

data.title: Oracle to increase restructuring costs by $500M; Citi reassured by results, guidance
data.description:
• In conjunction with its strong quarterly results and outlook, Oracle (ORCL) disclosed that it will spend $2.1B on restructuring costs in fiscal 2026, $500M more than previously stated.
  • The IT giant said in a filing with the Securities and Exchange Commission that the total estimated restructuring costs will be $2.1B. Additionally, it has already recorded $156M and $982M of restructuring costs in connection with the plan for the three and nine months ended Feb. 28, respectively. In December, Oracle estimated its total restructuring costs would be $1.6B. 
  • Oracle is implementing the layoffs, which are impacting thousands of employees, due in part to its immense spending on expanding data center capacity for artificial intelligence.
  • Separately, Citi analysts said on Thursday that the quarterly results were “reassuring” and helped “refute ongoing concerns around financing/data center delays.” As such, the firm reiterated its Buy rating on Oracle and raised its price target to $320 from $310.

data.title: Cion Investment GAAP NII of $0.35 beats by $0.01, TII of $53.79M beats by $2.02M
data.description:
• Cion Investment press release (CION): Q4 GAAP NII of $0.35 beats by $0.01.
  • Total investment income of $53.79M (-7.1% Y/Y) beats by $2.02M.
  • Net asset value per share was $13.76 as of December 31, 2025 compared to $14.86 as of September 30, 2025, a decrease of $1.10 per share, or 7.4%.

data.title: Calumet announces $150M additional 9.75% senior notes due 2031 in private placement
data.description:
• Calumet (CLMT) on Thursday said its subsidiaries Calumet Specialty Products Partners and Calumet Finance intend to offer $150M of 9.75% senior notes due 2031 in a private placement.
  • The net proceeds from the offering will be used to repay borrowings under its revolving credit facility.
  • The company said the additional notes will form part of its existing 9.75% senior notes due 2031, of which $405M were issued on Jan. 12, 2026, and will have the same terms except for the initial offering price.

data.title: Norsk Hydro to maintain scaled-down production at Qatar aluminum plant
data.description:
Norsk Hydro (NHYDY) (NHYKF) said Thursday its Qatalum 50-50 joint venture with Qatar Aluminum no longer plans to fully shut down its smelter in the country and will maintain operations at ~60% of capacity following confirmation from its gas supplier that the plant will continue to receive supply at reduced levels.

Qatalum began a controlled shutdown of the facility on March 3 after QatarEnergy said it planned to suspend gas supply, but QatarEnergy has since confirmed it will maintain gas supply at a reduced rate until further notice.

"Together with continuing operation at around 60%, this improves conditions for a future restart," Norsk Hydro (NHYDY) (NHYKF) said.

Qatalum was the first Middle East smelter to announce a disruption to its underlying operations, but traders and analysts have warned of more widespread curtailments unless the Strait of Hormuz reopens soon.

Aluminum prices (LMAHDS03:COM) have surged since the Iran war began, and futures on the London Metal Exchange rose Thursday to the highest in nearly four years before paring some gains, recently trading +0.4% after earlier rallying as much as 2.6% to $3,546.50/ton, the highest level since March 2022.

Aluminum looks poised to move toward $3,700/ton, with the spike in premiums in the U.S. and Europe reflecting "mounting concern among western buyers," BMI, a unit of Fitch Solutions Inc., said in a note.

"Recent developments materially heighten the likelihood of a more acute supply squeeze," with the global market expected to move into a deeper deficit of 1.06M tons this year, BMI said.

data.title: Joby Aviation takes a major step toward FAA certification
data.description:
Joby Aviation (JOBY) announced on Thursday that the eVTOL company has begun flight testing its first FAA-conforming electric air taxi aircraft. The development was called a key milestone toward FAA type certification and eventual commercial passenger service. 

Initial flights are being conducted by Joby (JOBY) pilots at the company's Marina, California test facility and are intended to pave the way for FAA pilots to start “for credit” Type Inspection Authorization testing later in 2026. The aircraft, designated N547JX, is the first in a fleet being built to support TIA and has been assembled using FAA-approved designs and components signed off under Joby’s FAA-approved test plans.

 

 

The eVTOL progress follows recent U.S. government clearance for mature designs like Joby's (JOBY) to begin early operations this year. Joby (JOBY) expects opportunities to fly in a wide range of states, including Arizona, Florida, New Jersey, New York, Texas, and others, which could significantly accelerate its path to commercial service. Joby (JOBY) attributes its speed from prototype to conforming aircraft to its vertically integrated model, under which it designs, engineers, tests, and manufactures most components in-house, reducing reliance on suppliers and tightening quality control and lead times.

Shares of Joby (JOBY) slipped 1.1% in premarket trading. The eVTOL stock is down more than 20% on a year-to-date basis.
